free_text: Production of Se-73 via (p,3n) and (d,4n) reactions
on arsenic.
high energy isochronous cyclotron
(JULIC) used  for incident proton energy of 45 MeV and
incident deuteron energy of 56 MeV
For the determination of excitation functions of the
(d,xn) reactions four stacks were irradiated at 50 nA
for 20 min with deuterons having a primary
energy of 56 MeV
compact cyclotron CV 28 used for
measurement up to 20 MeV.
35 cm3 Ge(Li) detector coupled with a Nuclear
Data 66 system
Thin arsenic layers were prepared by electrolytic
deposition of arsenic using an alkaline bath in a
mini-electrolytic cell. Al and Cu foils (50 and 30. mum
thick and of 16 mm diam.) were used as backing material
Total uncertainty was  estimated by taking
square roots of the sums of the squares of the
individual errors. On average, the error in cross
sections amounted to 16%. Total error includes:
* The error in the energy degradation is 0.5 MeV at
E(p) and E(d)=15 MeV and about 0.3 MeV at higher
energies
The error in the thickness of each foil and
arsenic layer is 4%
The error in the beam current measurement
Uncertainty in absolute activity
measurement of the products  (5-15)%
No description for the error bars is given.
SD:Updated to new date formats, lower case.
ANALYSIS deleted. Major corrections in all Subents.
BIB updated. MONITOR, DECAY-MON, REL-REF, part of
METHOD moved to Subents 002-007. ERR-ANALYS updated.
Four stacks (each consisting of electroplated
foils, several absorbers and beam current monitor
foils) were irradiated for 20 min at 50nA with an
incident proton energy of 45 MeV.
- Three stacks were irradiated at the CV28.
